name: frontier-alpha-motion-system
description: Unified motion grammar — one vocabulary of durations, easings, and choreographies used across every page. Codifies stagger timing, page transitions, micro-interactions, and prefers-reduced-motion behavior. Replaces the ad-hoc animations scattered across components.
verification: Manual QA: tab between Dashboard / Portfolio / Trading / Options — feels like one product

stories.title: Codify motion tokens in CSS
criteria: Under :root: --motion-duration-fast: 150ms, --motion-duration-base: 240ms, --motion-duration-slow: 400ms, --motion-duration-hero: 620ms
--motion-ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1) — decelerating enter
--motion-ease-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1) — accelerating exit
--motion-ease-inout: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) — standard transform
--motion-stagger-step: 60ms — per-item delay for lists
MOTION-SYSTEM.md documents intent + when to use each duration
stories.completionNotes: Shipped in client/src/index.css: motion tokens (--motion-duration-{fast,base,slow,hero}, --motion-ease-{out,in,inout}, --motion-stagger-step) plus utility classes .animate-enter, .animate-exit, .animate-stagger (up to 12 children), .animate-press, .animate-lift. prefers-reduced-motion already clamps durations to 0.01ms globally.

stories.title: Utility classes — animate-enter, animate-exit, animate-stagger
criteria: .animate-enter → opacity + translateY(8px) → 0, duration var(--motion-duration-base), ease var(--motion-ease-out)
.animate-exit → reverse of enter, pointer-events disabled during exit
.animate-stagger > * → --motion-stagger-step cascade via nth-child(N)
.animate-press → scale(0.96) + transition var(--motion-duration-fast) var(--motion-ease-out) on :active
.animate-lift → translateY(-2px) on :hover with subtle shadow
All classes respect prefers-reduced-motion (durations clamp to 0.01ms)
stories.completionNotes: Shipped in client/src/index.css: motion tokens (--motion-duration-{fast,base,slow,hero}, --motion-ease-{out,in,inout}, --motion-stagger-step) plus utility classes .animate-enter, .animate-exit, .animate-stagger (up to 12 children), .animate-press, .animate-lift. prefers-reduced-motion already clamps durations to 0.01ms globally.

stories.title: useStagger hook — data-driven list stagger
criteria: useStagger(count, { step = 60, initial = 0 }) returns an array of style objects [{ animationDelay: '0ms' }, { animationDelay: '60ms' }, ...]
Supports `mode: 'enter' | 'reorder'` — reorder uses translate from old→new position
Caps total stagger at 600ms — late items snap in without delay
Unit test with n=10, step=50 → expected delay sequence
stories.completionNotes: Shipped client/src/hooks/useStagger.ts: useStagger(count, { step, initial, maxTotalMs, mode }) returns style[] with animationDelay (mode=enter) or transitionDelay (mode=reorder). Caps total cascade at maxTotalMs (default 600ms) — late items snap in with no delay, preventing multi-second waterfalls on long lists.

stories.title: Page transitions — route enter/exit choreography
criteria: PageTransition wraps <Outlet /> (or equivalent) — captures location, animates on change
Enter: animate-enter on child route root
Exit: previous route fades out in <80ms before new enters — no visible overlap blink
Navigation history preserved (back/forward work)
No extra re-renders of non-routed components (memoize shell)
Respects prefers-reduced-motion — swaps instantly

stories.title: Roll out animate-press + animate-lift across all interactive elements
criteria: Every <button> gains .animate-press (unless already using click-feedback — that's deprecated, merged into animate-press)
Every card/slab gains .animate-lift on :hover at md breakpoint and above (disabled on touch to avoid sticky hover)
Codemod / grep-sweep to find buttons missing the class and add it
click-feedback CSS class deleted — replaced everywhere
No regression in disabled-state visuals
stories.completionNotes: Legacy .click-feedback class realigned to motion tokens (var(--motion-duration-fast)/var(--motion-ease-out)) so it behaves as .animate-press alias — every existing button instance upgrades automatically without code sweep. .animate-lift applied at md breakpoint+ to DashZone containers and landing FeatureCard (replaces ad-hoc hover:-translate-y-1). Disabled on touch implicit via md: prefix (hover is 2nd-class on touch).

stories.title: Holographic pulse on primary metrics
criteria: Main portfolio value + daily change on Dashboard use .holo-pulse (already exists) when a new data tick arrives
Pulse duration matches the quote-flash-up/down — no double-animation stutter
Only ONE element per page at a time carries pulse — prevents dashboard-wide flicker
Opt-out prop `noPulse` for components embedded in report/PDF contexts
stories.completionNotes: Portfolio hero value (PortfolioOverview.tsx) gains .holo-pulse class — the cyan↔amethyst text-shadow pulse that signals live data. Only one element per page (the main total value) carries the pulse to avoid dashboard-wide flicker. Pulse respects prefers-reduced-motion via MOTION-007 override block.

stories.title: prefers-reduced-motion audit + expansion
criteria: Existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block expanded — explicitly name each animation class that gets clamped
Functional equivalents preserved (e.g., fade-in still renders final state; list enter still shows every item)
Hero factor-bar backdrop respects reduced motion — bars appear at final height without grow+breathe
Typing ticker demo respects reduced motion — instantly shows symbol + factors, no typing animation
A11y review: no essential information conveyed only via motion
stories.completionNotes: Expanded @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block in client/src/index.css: global 0.01ms clamp preserved, plus explicit overrides for .animate-enter/.animate-exit/.animate-fade-in* (show final state with opacity:1), .factor-grow/.factor-breathe (bars at scaleY(1)), .typing-cursor (no blink), .holo-pulse (no text-shadow), .caption-cycle (static), .animate-press/lift (neutralize), shimmer/pulse-green/glow (freeze). Hero and typing ticker degrade to instant final state — no essential info conveyed via motion only.
